lorehq
─[2]-Primitives──

─[3]-Guides──
CommandsCustomizationGuidesPlatform SupportWorking Across Repos
─[4]-Workflow──
Active Work Architecture
─[ Fix Grouping and AI Selection ]─ ┐

Fix Grouping and AI Selection

The most novel component of the pipeline. When multiple field fixes target the same root cause, AI compares implementations and recommends the best one. Human maintainer makes the final merge decision.

Goal

When N instances independently fix the same bug, automatically group the fixes, compare them across quality dimensions, and surface a recommendation — turning a pile of duplicate PRs into a curated selection with rationale.

Approach

Grouping

Build on Phase 3’s duplicate detection with deeper similarity analysis:

  1. Semantic similarity — Compare PR diffs and issue descriptions using embeddings
  2. Component clustering — Fixes touching the same files with overlapping root cause descriptions
  3. Parent issue — Group related fixes under a single tracking issue (created automatically or linked manually)

AI Comparison

When a group has 2+ fixes, trigger a comparison workflow:

Scoring dimensions:

DimensionWeightDescription
MinimalityHighFewer lines changed, less surface area for regressions
Cross-platform coverageHighWorks on Linux + macOS + Windows vs. single platform
Test evidenceMediumIncludes before/after verification, covers edge cases
Convention adherenceMediumPasses all required review criteria from Phase 4
Code qualityMediumReadability, idiomatic patterns, error handling
Root cause depthLowAddresses the underlying cause vs. symptom

Output: Summary comment on the parent issue:

## Fix Comparison: [Bug Description]

**3 field fixes submitted.** Recommended: PR #42.

| PR | Author | Minimality | Cross-platform | Tests | Conventions | Quality | Score |
|----|--------|-----------|----------------|-------|-------------|---------|-------|
| #42 | instance-a | 5/5 | 5/5 | 4/5 | 5/5 | 4/5 | 23/25 |
| #38 | instance-b | 3/5 | 5/5 | 3/5 | 4/5 | 3/5 | 18/25 |
| #45 | instance-c | 4/5 | 2/5 | 5/5 | 5/5 | 4/5 | 20/25 |

### Rationale
PR #42 is the most minimal fix (3 lines changed vs 8 and 12) while maintaining
full cross-platform compatibility. PR #45 has stronger test evidence but uses a
macOS-only sed pattern that would need adaptation.

### Synthesis opportunity
PR #42's implementation with PR #45's test coverage would be the ideal merge.
Consider cherry-picking test additions from #45 after merging #42.

Embedding Infrastructure

Evaluate in priority order (cheapest viable option first):

  1. Heuristic grouping (MVP) — Token overlap on root cause + file path matching. No external dependencies. Ship this first.
  2. Local FastEmbed — Already used by lore-docker for semantic search. Could reuse the same model for PR similarity. See fastembed-local-files-only skill for configuration.
  3. Anthropic API embeddings — Higher quality, but adds API cost per comparison. Reserve for production scale.
  4. Qdrant-based similarity — Full vector DB. Overkill unless fix volume is high (50+ fixes/month).

Scope

Target repo: /home/andrew/Github/lore (source framework)

Files to create:

  • .github/workflows/field-fix-compare.yml — comparison workflow, triggered when a group reaches 2+ fixes
  • .github/prompts/field-fix-compare.md — prompt template for the comparison
  • .github/scripts/group-field-fixes.js — grouping logic (heuristic MVP)

Dependencies

  • Phase 3 (intake triage) — provides the initial duplicate detection signals
  • Phase 4 (AI review gate) — convention adherence scores feed into the comparison

Deliverables

  • Grouping logic detects and clusters related field fixes
  • Parent issue is created or updated when a group forms
  • AI comparison runs when a group reaches 2+ fixes
  • Structured comparison comment is posted on the parent issue
  • Synthesis opportunities are identified when fixes have complementary strengths

Success Criteria

  • Two PRs fixing the same bug are automatically grouped
  • Comparison table scores are consistent with human judgment (validate on 5+ examples)
  • Recommended fix is the one a human maintainer would also choose (>80% agreement)
  • Synthesis suggestions are actionable (point to specific commits/lines to cherry-pick)
  • Grouping doesn’t produce false positives (unrelated fixes grouped together)

Open Questions

  1. Group threshold — Should comparison trigger at 2 fixes or wait for 3+? Start at 2 for faster feedback.
  2. Score calibration — Weights are initial guesses. Calibrate after the first 10 real comparisons.
  3. Partial merges — Can the pipeline suggest merging parts of different PRs? The “synthesis opportunity” field is a start, but automated cherry-pick is risky.
  4. Stale fixes — How long should a group stay open? Auto-close after 30 days of inactivity?

Test Plan

# Create two PRs that fix the same bug differently
# Verify grouping detects them as related
# Verify comparison comment appears on parent issue

# Create a third PR with a clearly inferior fix
# Verify it scores lower in the comparison
